objective: Implement a thin CLI delegation surface, runtime templates, architecture documentation, and integration tests from P1-SPEC-01 and P1-SPEC-08 without adding hidden business rules.
implemented_capabilities: Thin CLI entrypoint with candidate-check, runtime-boundary-check, and workpack-readiness commands.
CLI candidate-check delegates to candidate state, output coverage, and optional review binding validators.
CLI runtime-boundary-check delegates to repository runtime import and runtime read boundary validators.
CLI workpack-readiness delegates to workpack readiness state and scope validators.
Review decision and stage manifest JSON templates under repository-local templates.
Architecture documentation describing CLI and template authority boundaries.
Integration smoke tests for CLI command behavior and validator delegation.
Integration static thinness tests that reject CLI-local domain classes, graph traversal, compiler conservation logic, promotion constructors, review constructors, and lock constructors.
Review repair: CLI input failures now return structured JSON findings instead of uncaught exceptions.
Review repair: candidate subject hashing now runs only after required output validation passes.
Review repair: runtime read boundary validation now checks actual Path/open call sites instead of strategy constants or plain text literals.
Review repair: static CLI import checks now inspect both import and from-import syntax.

deferred_due_scope.reason: P1-IMPLEMENT-09 is scoped to CLI, templates, docs, and integration tests; the review repair only adjusted the existing foundation boundary helper that the CLI delegates to.
No package metadata file is governed by this workpack; the module entrypoint is executable through python3 -m drd_harness.cli.main when repository/src is on PYTHONPATH.

deferred_due_scope.item: Human Gate final approval and BUILD_LOCK creation
deferred_due_scope.reason: The CLI cannot approve, promote, or lock its own candidate output.
